headline: French sociologist, philosopher and intellectual provocateur Edgar Morin has died aged 104
description: Committed to connecting different fields of knowledge to develop his 'complex thought,' this former member of the Resistance and deeply humanist intellectual advocated for a revolution of consciousness. He died in Paris on Friday, his wife confirmed to Le Monde.

headline: PSG-Arsenal: Paris braces for a tense weekend with Champions League final and several large concerts
The Bolloré protégée spreading Russian propaganda in France
France heatwave sparks calls for more supervision at swimming areas after multiple drownings
description: At least eight people have died by drowning in recent days. While some municipalities are trying to adapt their supervision and safety measures, many lack the capacity, given the number of locations involved – which are not always authorized for swimming – and the shortage of lifeguards.

headline: Aya Nakamura returns to the stage to claim her crown as the queen of the Stade de France
description: The singer put on a nearly three-hour show in front of 70,000 fans during the first of her three Paris concerts.

headline: The little-known story of Bella Ariel, a Lanvin model murdered at Auschwitz
description: Journalist and author Arnaud Nemet tells the story of his great-grand-aunt, Bella Ariel, a celebrated 1930s model whose life was both dazzling and tragic. A Jewish immigrant from Constantinople, she was arrested after a denunciation and murdered at Auschwitz-Birkenau at 31.

headline: French Open 2026: Moïse Kouamé's meteoric rise
description: Poised for greatness, the 17-year-old French player has won his first two matches at the Paris Grand Slam and faces Chilean Alejandro Tabilo on Saturday in the third round. It is a spectacular ascent for a player already recognized as a young phenomenon.

Iran is gradually reconnecting to the internet, though access remains limited
description: After a three-month blackout, Iran regained partial internet access on Tuesday, May 26. The Intelligence Ministry has warned Iranians against collaborating with any foreign media, which it accused of espionage.

headline: 'Reckless, racist, incompetent and treasonous': Bruce Springsteen delivers scathing indictment of Trump at Washington concert
description: During the final concert of his US tour, the 76-year-old artist accused the Republican president of 'destroying the American idea and our reputation around the world.'

headline: Europe's heatwave in pictures, from Milan's Duomo to the crowded beaches of southern England
description: Europe is currently experiencing an unprecedented heatwave. From Italy to the United Kingdom, several countries have recorded temperatures far above seasonal norms, prompting some authorities to issue the highest-level health alerts.
